scenario_description: MOCK platform analytics shaped like anonymized player behavior aggregates. No real player data is used.
common_misconceptions: Players assume the piano puzzle is self-contained and do not look at the mirror
Players try random note combinations instead of seeking environmental clues in the room
Players who lit the candle believe they have completed the room and leave

effective_hint_patterns.framing: Look at the mirror while playing.
The mirror and piano are connected. Try adjusting the mirror first.
Not everything in this room is decorative. Some objects listen.

representative_player_quotes: I spent 20 minutes on the piano because I didn't realize the mirror was part of the same puzzle.
The candle inscription says 'where the notes land, the glass remembers' but I thought that was just flavor text.
Once I moved the mirror to face the piano the last two notes clicked immediately.
